title: Heat & Temperature: Understanding Energy Transfer
summary: This video explores heat as a form of energy transfer due to temperature differences and explains how objects with different temperatures interact. By understanding the concepts, we can grasp important phenomena in everyday life.

sections.heading: What is Heat?
sections.body: Heat is energy that flows between two objects or substances due to a difference in temperature. It's not something you can see but can certainly feel and measure.
key_points: Heat as invisible form of energy
Energy flow from higher to lower temperatures

sections.heading: Understanding Temperature
sections.body: Temperature is a measurement that indicates how hot or cold an object feels. Unlike heat, which depends on the rate of energy transfer, temperature measures internal kinetic activity.
key_points: Internal movement relates to feeling warmth/coldness

sections.heading: Thermal Equilibrium and Conduction
sections.body: When two objects at different temperatures come into contact, they will exchange heat until both reach the same temperature. This state is called thermal equilibrium.
key_points: Heat flows to cooler object in warming process
Equilibrium when no more net energy transfer

key_facts: Temperature measured in Kelvin or Celsi0us
